Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Megadev: A Development Kit for the Sega Mega Drive and Mega CD Hardware 86 upvotes, 5 comments
  2. Baochip-1x: What it is, why I'm doing it now and how it came about 207 upvotes, 26 comments
  3. Can I run AI locally? 1353 upvotes, 323 comments
  4. NMAP in the Movies 66 upvotes, 9 comments
  5. Show HN: GitAgent – An open standard that turns any Git repo into an AI agent 25 upvotes, 2 comments
  6. Secure Secrets Management for Cursor Cloud Agents 34 upvotes, 5 comments
  7. Cookie jars capture American kitsch (2023) 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  8. Mouser: An open source alternative to Logi-Plus mouse software 402 upvotes, 133 comments
  9. I found 39 Algolia admin keys exposed across open source documentation sites 148 upvotes, 44 comments
  10. Python: The Optimization Ladder 167 upvotes, 51 comments
  11. Atari 2600 BASIC Programming (2015) 51 upvotes, 13 comments
  12. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 1034 upvotes, 431 comments
  13. Philosoph Jürgen Habermas Gestorben 97 upvotes, 33 comments
  14. Show HN: Ink – Deploy full-stack apps from AI agents via MCP or Skills 18 upvotes, 3 comments
  15. Online astroturfing: A problem beyond disinformation 37 upvotes, 10 comments
  16. The Isolation Trap: Erlang 120 upvotes, 48 comments
  17. What happens when US economic data becomes unreliable 89 upvotes, 36 comments
  18. A Survival Guide to a PhD (2016) 154 upvotes, 93 comments
  19. RAM kits are now sold with one fake RAM stick alongside a real one 183 upvotes, 127 comments
  20. Montana passes Right to Compute act (2025) 158 upvotes, 111 comments
  21. Nominal Types in WebAssembly 26 upvotes, 12 comments
  22. Recursive Problems Benefit from Recursive Solutions 42 upvotes, 23 comments
  23. Everything you never wanted to know about visually-hidden 11 upvotes, 3 comments
  24. An Ode to Bzip 19 upvotes, 10 comments
  25. Digg is gone again 331 upvotes, 346 comments
  26. XML Is a Cheap DSL 188 upvotes, 190 comments
  27. Show HN: Learn Arabic with spaced repetition and comprehensible input 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. The $2 per hour worker behind the OnlyFans boom 7 upvotes, 3 comments
  29. Wired headphone sales are exploding 335 upvotes, 566 comments
  30. 9 Mothers Defense (YC P26) Is Hiring in Austin 0 upvotes, 0 comments
